_comment: Labelled precision/recall corpus for EnumGrid's OFFLINE version->CVE matcher
(backend/vulndb.lookup_offline_cves). Each case pins the EXACT set of CVE ids the
matcher must return for a given service banner, so cve_precision.py can compute
true precision AND recall (closed-world: any id returned that is not in 'expect'
is a false positive; any 'expect' id not returned is a false negative).
exact         - a textbook-vulnerable build; the CVE MUST be recalled.
boundary      - the version sits just outside a vulnerable range (fixed build);
the CVE MUST NOT be reported (tests range edges).
wrong-product - the version number collides with a DIFFERENT product's magic
version, or a product name is a substring of another; the CVE
MUST be attributed to the right product only.
backport      - a distro build whose version is not the vulnerable upstream
build; the exact-match table MUST NOT over-report.
control       - no known-vulnerable build / no version; MUST return nothing.
The matcher scores against UPSTREAM-version semantics. Where a distro backports a
fix without bumping the version (e.g. Ubuntu OpenSSH 8.9p1 vs regreSSHion), the
offline table over-reports by design and flags 'verify'; that residual is
suppressed by the backport-aware OSV layer (osv.py). See docs/ACCURACY.md.

cases.note: regreSSHion, upstream 8.5p1-9.7p1. On a backport-patched distro OSV suppresses this.
upper edge of the regreSSHion range (inclusive)
regreSSHion fixed in 9.8p1 - must NOT match
enum fixed at 7.7 - must NOT match
RHEL/CentOS backport build (httpd-2.4.6-97.el7); not the vulnerable 2.4.49/2.4.50 build
one past the vulnerable range upper bound (1.3.9)
REGRESSION GUARD: 'httpd' is a substring of 'lighttpd'; must NOT inherit Apache CVE-2021-41773
Windows/IIS 6.0 WebDAV; 'httpd' token present but version is not an Apache build
Tomcat connector banner; version 1.1 is outside every Ghostcat range and is not an Apache httpd build
version 2.3.4 is vsftpd's backdoor magic version, but on OpenSSH it must map to the OpenSSH CVE, never vsftpd's CVE-2011-2523
2.3.4 must not trigger vsftpd's CVE-2011-2523 on nginx, and is outside nginx's own range
not in the curated table - must return nothing, never guess
no version detected - must not guess a CVE
